ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03411433 covers lobster food product broadly; no specific entry for whole steamed lobster at medium weight exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CJW covers lobster as a commodity; no preparation-specific (steamed, whole) sub-code available. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227144007 identifies lobster as a substance; no cooked/steamed preparation variant exists in SNOMED CT. Closest parent used.

serving.description: 2 whole medium lobsters (500–600 g each), approximately 1000–1200 g combined live weight; edible yield approximately 35–40%, giving ~380–460 g edible cooked meat
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving consists of two medium whole steamed lobsters each weighing 500–600 g live weight, yielding approximately 420 g of edible cooked lobster meat shared between diners.

nutrient_source.title: Lobster, cooked, moist heat (NDB No. 15147) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked lobster (moist heat) used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent cooked edible lobster meat per 100 g. The restaurant preparation (steamed whole lobster) is consistent with moist-heat cooking. Sodium value reflects plain steamed lobster without added salt sauce; restaurant preparations may add soy-based dipping sauce which would increase sodium. Serving size estimated from 500–600 g live weight per lobster × 2, with ~35–40% edible yield.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal, American Diabetes Association)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for steamed whole lobster. Lobster is a high-protein, very low-carbohydrate food (0.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g). By analogy with other cooked crustaceans and high-protein seafoods documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, GI is estimated at approximately 5 (effectively negligible), consistent with the principle that foods with negligible available carbohydrate have a very low GI. Glycemic load per 420 g serving = (5 × 2.1 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 0.1, rounded to 0.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from natural lobster and potential added soy-based dipping sauces
hypertension.guidance: Plain steamed lobster contains approximately 380 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should request minimal or no added soy sauce or salt-based condiments, and limit portion to one lobster to keep sodium intake within meal targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content and very low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Steamed lobster contains only 0.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g with a negligible glycemic index (~5), making it an excellent high-protein, low-glycemic choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided dipping sauces with added sugar are avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated dietary cholesterol (145 mg per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lobster provides lean protein and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids but contains approximately 145 mg cholesterol per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume in moderation and avoid high-sodium or butter-based accompaniments to limit saturated fat and sodium load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Combo Lobster (Medium, 2 Pieces) is a premium Singaporean Chinese seafood dish consisting of two whole live lobsters each weighing 500–600 g, typically prepared by steaming to preserve the natural sweetness and delicate texture of the meat. The dish is a signature offering at seafood-focused Chinese restaurants in Singapore, such as those specialising in white bee hoon and zi char cuisine. Steaming is the preferred cooking method as it retains the lobster's natural umami flavour, produces tender and juicy flesh, and avoids the addition of excess fat. The cooked meat is pale white to cream-coloured, firm yet succulent, with a mildly sweet oceanic flavour. Nutritionally, steamed lobster is a high-protein, very low-fat, low-carbohydrate food providing approximately 98 kcal, 20.5 g protein, and only 0.9 g fat per 100 g of edible meat. It is an excellent source of selenium, zinc, vitamin B12, phosphorus, and potassium. The glycemic index is negligible (estimated ~5), making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate at ~380 mg per 100 g from the lobster itself; dipping sauces such as soy sauce, ginger-scallion sauce, or garlic butter can significantly increase sodium and fat intake. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. It is a major crustacean allergen and must be avoided by individuals with shellfish allergy. Cholesterol content (~145 mg per 100 g) warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease. This dish is typically served as a shared centrepiece at family-style Chinese seafood meals and is associated with celebratory dining occasions in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in steamed lobster?
Is steamed lobster high in sodium?
Is lobster healthy for heart disease?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA SR Legacy for cooked lobster (moist heat), which is a strong match for steamed whole lobster. However, the exact restaurant preparation at The Seafood Market (By House of White Bee Hoon) may include sauces or seasonings not reflected in the plain steamed values. Serving size is estimated from live weight and edible yield ratio. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct measurement for steamed lobster exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are based on USDA SR Legacy 'Lobster, cooked, moist heat' per 100 g edible portion. This is the closest approved-source match for plain steamed whole lobster. 2. Serving size of 420 g represents the estimated edible yield from two medium lobsters (500–600 g live weight each) at approximately 35–40% edible yield. 3. Sodium value (380 mg/100 g) reflects the natural sodium content of cooked lobster without added condiments; actual sodium intake may be higher if soy sauce or other dipping sauces are consumed. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked lobster; naturally zero as lobster contains no industrially produced trans fats. 5. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as lobster contains no free sugars. 6. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; naturally zero as lobster is an animal product containing no dietary fibre. 7. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked lobster. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked lobster. 9. GI is estimated at 5 based on analogy with other high-protein, very low-carbohydrate crustaceans; no direct GI measurement for steamed lobster exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or Atkinson et al. 2008. 10. Halal status is marked false as lobster is considered non-halal (haram) under the Shafi'i school of Islamic jurisprudence followed by the majority of Malay Muslims in Singapore; status may differ under other madhabs. 11. FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes use closest_parent match type as no preparation-specific steamed lobster codes exist in these ontologies. 12. Iteration 3 fix: aliases consistency audit performed. All strings present in aliases_by_language verified verbatim in flat aliases list. The romanized entry 'Qīng zhēng lóngxiā' added to flat aliases to match its presence in aliases_by_language.romanized. Hindi entries confirmed as pure Devanagari script in both aliases_by_language and flat aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from USDA SR Legacy source for cooked lobster. Added all mandatory multilingual names (Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Documented all zero values (trans_fat, sugar, fibre, vitamin_c) with confirmed-zero justification in notes. Set vitamin_d_mcg to -1 with explicit documentation. Estimated GI at 5 using analogous crustacean evidence from Atkinson et al. 2008. Applied closest_parent match type for all three ontology anchors with gap notes.

Iteration 2: Addressed q8 failure reported by validator. Corrected mixed-script Hindi entry in aliases_by_language to pure Devanagari. Verified flat aliases list contained matching pure Devanagari strings.

Iteration 3: Performed exhaustive aliases consistency audit. Enumerated every string in aliases_by_language across all language keys. Identified that 'Qīng zhēng lóngxiā' was present in aliases_by_language.romanized but absent from flat aliases list. Added 'Qīng zhēng lóngxiā' to flat aliases. Confirmed all 25 aliases_by_language string values are now present verbatim in flat aliases. Script purity verified for all non-Latin entries.
